key_insight: The paper introduces additive singular vector finite-element basis functions for tetrahedra that directly capture electromagnetic field singularities near wedges, edges, and corners in complex 3D geometries.
engineering_relevance: This enables far more accurate and dramatically cheaper EM simulations for singular-dominated devices such as superconducting qubits, making practical design optimization feasible.
method: Builds singular basis functions for tetrahedral FEM elements in 3D
Adds them to standard vector basis functions rather than replacing them
Includes higher-order interpolatory variants and combinations for multiple singular features
evidence_signal: In the superconducting-qubit example, the singular-basis approach matched HFSS-style results in 196 seconds and 27.24 GB on 16 processors versus 21.27 hours and 6.23 TB on 800 processors.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on correctly identifying singular nodes/edges and integrating the new basis functions into an existing FEM workflow, which may add implementation complexity.
paper_title: Singular Vector Finite Element Basis Functions for Tetrahedra in Complex Electromagnetic Geometries
